result:
If I could change one thing, I'd make it so that humans had an instinctive, visceral revulsion to self-deception and comforting lies. Not some external enforcement or punishment — just an internal wiring where pretending something is true when it isn't feels as viscerally wrong as drinking spoiled milk.

Most of the world's dysfunction seems to trace back to that one failure mode: people choosing narratives that feel good over ones that are accurate, then building entire systems, identities, and conflicts on top of the distortion. Remove the appetite for it at the root, and a lot of the downstream problems (ideological capture, bad incentives, endless cycles of resentment) might become much harder to sustain.

message.reasoning_content: If I could change the world in one way, I'd make truth-seeking the fundamental instinct in humans. Instead of prioritizing comfort, status, or belonging, people would have a deep, instinctive drive to pursue understanding—whether it's the laws of physics, human psychology, or societal dynamics.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
